pages.text: The metal chain has three stages. &6Early Prospecting&r reveals ores in the rock; &6Ore Mining&r sets miners to work the veins; and &6Smelting&r draws the metal out with heat and the bellows.

Smelting is done at the &6Bloomery&r, an early furnace built from mud bricks and a block of coal. Fire sticks light it, and pumping the bellows keeps it hot enough to turn raw iron and copper into ingots. Raw tin feeds the crucible toward bronze.
Iron Working is the capstone: it unlocks iron tools, armor, and shields, and advances your settlement to the Medieval era. Reaching iron is a major turning point in a civilization's life.
